title: Theoretical Physics Brain (01 Theoretical Physics)
purpose: Register the Obsidian folder `obsidian-markdown/01 Theoretical Physics` as the operator-primary, human-readable downstream realization of the theoretical-physics knowledge network inside a working clone.

Support evidence-led formal-theory promotion packets that land in canonical notes while staying semantically aligned with the paired `backend:theoretical-physics-knowledge-network` structured backend.
artifact_granularity: One canonical note, or one tightly coupled note bundle declared by the local domain index and unresolved queue, is the atomic human-facing backend artifact.
retrieval_hints: Start from the domain index, existing canonical notes, and unresolved queues in `obsidian-markdown/01 Theoretical Physics` before creating a new note.
Require explicit evidence anchors, assumptions, regime limits, and reusable identity before treating a note as promotion-ready.
Treat folder layout as navigation rather than ontology; reusable identity must still be written explicitly in the note and promotion packet.

When the paired `backend:theoretical-physics-knowledge-network` backend is also active, keep semantic identity, source anchors, assumptions, regime limits, and unresolved-gap boundaries aligned across the human-readable and typed surfaces.
Treat this backend as operator-primary for reading, curation, and domain narrative; that role does not make it globally more authoritative than the paired typed backend.
notes: This card promotes the active Obsidian theoretical-physics brain to a formal AITP backend. It is explicitly paired with `backend:theoretical-physics-knowledge-network`. Under the paired-backend pattern, the brain and the standalone TPKN repository are governed paired realizations of the same formal-theory knowledge network. The brain is operator-primary, TPKN is machine-primary, and neither backend gains silent authority from format alone. See `canonical/backends/THEORETICAL_PHYSICS_PAIRED_BACKEND_CONTRACT.md`.
